other_observers: Nathan Pieplow; first found and IDed by Nathan and myself
description: the bird was a large dove, slightly larger than a Mourning Dove, with a much bulkier shape, mostly due to a relatively larger head, and a shorter but wider tail. The bill was a typical dove bill, and black. The legs were dark flesh colored  The overall color of the dove was medium grayish, with a slight pinkish tinge on the underparts and head, and a brownish tinge on the mantle and wings. The bird had a bold but thin black collar, bordered by a very thin area of white. The underside of the tail was white, with black on the base of the outer primaries, on the outer webs.Behaviors: the birds were seen on the ground near a bird feeder, and one bird spent a good deal of time perched on a wireCall: none heardPlumage: adult
